report_name: Attackers Exploit Robinhood Account Creation Flaw to Send Phishing Emails from noreply@robinhood.com

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Attackers sent phishing emails containing a malicious link to a credential harvesting site, robinhood.casevaultreview.com. The emails appeared legitimate as they were sent from Robinhood's own servers.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: A phishing campaign was conducted by exploiting a vulnerability in Robinhood's account creation process, allowing attackers to send emails from the legitimate noreply@robinhood.com address to target customers.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploited an HTML injection vulnerability in the device metadata field of Robinhood's public-facing account creation workflow to embed malicious content in automated confirmation emails.

vulnerabilities.name: HTML Injection in Robinhood Account Creation
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Robinhood's account creation workflow where the 'Device' metadata field did not sanitize user-supplied input, allowing attackers to inject malicious HTML into automated new account confirmation emails.

identities.name: Robinhood
identities.description: The trading platform whose account creation process was exploited. Its legitimate email address, noreply@robinhood.com, was abused to send phishing emails.

identities.name: Robinhood Customers
identities.description: The intended victims of the phishing campaign designed to steal their account credentials.

infrastructure.name: Credential Harvesting Site
infrastructure.description: A phishing site hosted at robinhood.casevaultreview.com, designed to look like the legitimate Robinhood site to steal user credentials.

indicators.name: Robinhood Phishing Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Robinhood Phishing Domain DNS Query
id: 2f8e1c9a-7b0d-4f3e-8a1b-9c0d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the phishing domain used in the Robinhood HTML injection campaign.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/robinhood-account-creation-flaw-abused-to-send-phishing-emails/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'robinhood.casevaultreview.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the phishing domain robinhood.casevaultreview.com, which was used for credential harvesting in a campaign abusing a Robinhood vulnerability.

indicators.name: Robinhood Phishing Email
indicators.stix_pattern: [email-message:sender_ref.value = 'noreply@robinhood.com' AND email-message:subject = 'Your recent login to Robinhood']
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Robinhood Phishing Email Detection
id: 8d1b9c0d-2e3f-4a5b-6c7d-8e9f0a1b2c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects emails with a subject used in the Robinhood phishing campaign, originating from the legitimate but abused noreply@robinhood.com address.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/robinhood-account-creation-flaw-abused-to-send-phishing-emails/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1566
level: medium
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    Sender: 'noreply@robinhood.com'
    Subject|contains: 'Your recent login to Robinhood'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects emails with the subject 'Your recent login to Robinhood' sent from the abused 'noreply@robinhood.com' address. These emails contained malicious HTML injected to create a phishing lure.

relationships.target_name: HTML Injection in Robinhood Account Creation
